envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Risk Map intake identifies missing policies, missing approvals, overbroad memory scopes, high-risk tools, high-risk workflows, sensitive systems, blocked areas, unknown areas, and compliance-sensitive surfaces. Neutral compliance vocabulary per ADR-0070.

items.name: Risk Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Risk Map. Identifies governance + memory + tool + workflow + connector + surveillance + compliance risks the rest of the Map system must respect. Neutral compliance vocabulary per ADR-0070 — never claims regulator approval or guaranteed compliance.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks what's risky, what's missing, what's unknown. Outputs Risk Map candidates pending admin approval. Risks gate downstream activation; they never enable autonomous response.
items.model_usage_notes: Risk surfaces inform admin governance — never autonomous Twin response. Per ADR-0070: neutral compliance vocabulary mandatory. Per RULE-20: forbidden surveillance language absolute.
required_questions: What policies are missing or incomplete (acceptable use / data handling / vendor approval / connector use)?
What approvals are missing or undefined (spend / external commit / write actions)?
What memory scopes are currently overbroad (everyone-sees-everything risk)?
What tools carry high risk (legal-effect / money-moving / customer-PII)?
What workflows carry high risk (irreversible / external-effect / regulated)?
optional_questions: Which sensitive systems exist (HR / Legal / Finance / IT / M&A / R&D)?
Which areas are explicitly blocked by policy (no AI today)?
Which areas are unknown (no clear policy, no admin owner)?
What workforce-monitoring risk concerns exist? (Documented to be excluded by construction)
Which surfaces are compliance-sensitive (GDPR / HIPAA / FERPA / SOX / regional law)?
What connector risks apply (OAuth scope creep / token theft / overbroad scope)?
admin_questions: Which risks must the Map encode at first activation?
Which risks must block downstream Map activation entirely?
Which risks require ADR-0050 break-glass governance?
Which risks require ADR-0036 LawfulBasis at regulator-view tier?
What RULE-20-protected forbidden inferences must be encoded at the Map level?
user_questions: Does the Risk Map reflect risks you've already considered?
What risks would you want the system to surface to you on a recurring basis?
evidence_sources: Wave 2.1 role-depth files §28 (per-role risks and guardrails)
output_candidates: Risk Map proposal (missing policies / missing approvals / overbroad scope / high-risk tools+workflows / blocked areas / unknown areas / compliance surfaces)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every Risk Map region before downstream maps activate
Map-blocking risks gate Tool / Workflow / Connector / Memory Map activation
Neutral compliance vocabulary mandatory per ADR-0070
No claims of regulator approval, no claims of guaranteed compliance, no claims of absence of fine risk
RULE-20-protected forbidden inferences absolute regardless of plan tier
safe_defaults: Conservative default for unknown areas (treat as blocked until reviewed)
DMW_scope_implications: Risk Map gates which DMW scopes may activate downstream
Map-blocking risks exclude their domains from any DMW scope
Compliance-sensitive surfaces require LawfulBasis per ADR-0036 at regulator-view DMW scope
Unknown areas default to forbidden-DMW until reviewed
audit_expectations: future BREAK_GLASS_* + LawfulBasis emissions preserved per ADR-0050 + ADR-0036
